page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8ise.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture utilizing an Intel Xeon 8475B processor operating at a base frequency of 2.7 GHz. The instance is configured with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs, maintaining a strict one thread per core ratio. This dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance without resource sharing at the core level.

The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring the attachment of external block storage for operating system and application data. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 6 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given its balanced 1:4 CPU-to-memory ratio and dedicated compute resources, the ecs.g8ise.2xlarge is designed for general-purpose workloads. It is suitable for applications requiring predictable CPU performance, such as medium-scale web servers, application servers, containerized microservices, and development environments. The lack of bundled local storage and GPUs positions this instance as a cost-efficient option for workloads that rely on network-attached storage and do not require hardware acceleration.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g8ise.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance built on the KVM hypervisor and powered by an Intel Xeon 8475B processor running at 2.7 GHz. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, the instance provides 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs, ensuring a dedicated CPU allocation with one thread per core. It features 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached volumes. Network performance is anchored by a 6 Gbps baseline bandwidth. This hardware profile makes the instance highly suitable for general-purpose workloads, including application servers, containerized deployments, and web serving environments that demand predictable compute performance without the overhead of specialized hardware accelerators.
